comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: STATUS 2026-07-12 (/rebar-implement): CLAIMED (in_progress), plan HARDENED + signed through the claim gate, NOT yet implemented. This is one of the two largest kernel leaves — Pass-2 grounding hardening, 3 components: (1) absence-claim veto — add abstain-safe binaries claims_absence + absence_confirmed_in_context to the SHARED verification contract (verify.py, alongside cited_reference_accurate at ~L143; NOT in GRADED_BINARY) + a symmetric deterministic veto in decide.py (mirror the cited-reference veto at decide.py:319-327: claims_absence==yes AND absence_confirmed_in_context==no -> dropped, reason veto:absence-refuted); (2) DET-tier hygiene backstop at the DET emission point (det_floor.det_blocking_findings/det_advisory_findings @932/942 or partition_findings @orchestrator.py:380 — NOT pass3_over_findings, which DET findings bypass; P5 task-dag ALREADY emits subjects so it is NOT a fix target); (3) operator-attested AC awareness — DET-detect [operator-attested] and inject operator_attested into severity_attributes in plan_review/workflow_ops.py plan_review_decide (~L324), clearing ac_unverifiable from attrs BEFORE impact_plan reads it (impact_plan stays byte-unchanged). Contract test tests/unit/test_review_kernel.py:176-207 must be updated to include the 2 new binary fields. f1fa 11:44 verdict (norm ne7642adbf79 + G3 n0eb3f2e9da9) becomes the regression fixture. REMAINING: implement under held-out TDD (RED-first: absence-veto drop + additive-contract + DET-hygiene drop + operator-attested ac_unverifiable-clear), commit in the stack, push, close.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Bug 5e40 (auto-lander epic f1fa), verified from the store: a blocking E2/F1 finding with a FALSE absence premise ('the plan never tasks anyone with capturing the pre-cutover snapshot') survived Pass-2 at validity 0.94, picked up hard-override axes (impact 0.85), and blocked — while a G3 advisory in the SAME verdict quoted the very provision the block claimed absent (norm ne7642adbf79, 07-12 11:44 verdict; the plan text contains actor, artifact path infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json, and timing). Separately: DET findings fire contentless ('Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge' — naming no tickets/files, unadjudicable), and plan-review criteria are blind to ADR-0043's [operator-attested] tag, demanding in-session proving commands for ACs the completion gate accepts by recorded attestation. Design approved by operator 2026-07-12; each component preserves the four-pass separation (pass 1 discovers, pass 2 answers narrow binaries, pass 3 drops deterministically, pass 4 coaches non-prescriptively).

## What
COMPONENT 1 — absence-claim grounding veto:
- Pass 1: UNCHANGED (independence invariant, ADR 0008).
- Pass 2: add two abstain-safe binaries to the shared verification contract (src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py + the verifier prompts): `claims_absence` ('does this finding's premise assert something is missing/never specified/not tasked? yes/no' — classifies the FINDING text) and `absence_confirmed_in_context` ('search the plan text provided; is there any provision matching the item claimed absent? quote it if found; yes/no/insufficient' — a lookup against the context the verifier already re-grounds on). Missing answers abstain (no effect), like the plan-v2 axes.
- Pass 3: deterministic veto in src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py, symmetric to the existing cited-reference veto (decide.py:318-326): claims_absence == yes AND absence_confirmed_in_context == no -> dropped (premise-refuted). No threshold changes.
- Pass 4: unchanged.
COMPONENT 2 — DET finding content floor:
- DET content floor. CODE-GROUNDED CORRECTION: the original "sibling-overlap detector emits contentless findings" premise is STALE — the sibling-overlap emitter is the P5 task-dag interference branch (det_floor.py:491-509), and it ALREADY names subjects (its `evidence` is `_file_interference(...)` → `"{path}: {ticketA} & {ticketB} (no ordering edge)"`, i.e. path + ticket ids). There is no "P6-class sibling-overlap detector" (P6 is the AC/DD quality checker). So COMPONENT 2's substantive deliverable is the deterministic DET-tier HYGIENE BACKSTOP, not an emitter fix: an audit step confirms which DET emitters (if any) still omit subjects — and the change records that P5 already complies — while the enforceable guarantee is the backstop below (drop any subject-less DET finding). If the audit finds a genuinely subject-less emitter, fix it; otherwise the backstop stands alone as the safety net.
- Backstop: a deterministic hygiene rule SCOPED TO DET-TIER findings only — a DET finding with no location and no concrete evidence spans is unadjudicable and drops. IMPLEMENTATION HOOK: DET findings never enter `pass3_over_findings` (they flow through `det_blocking_findings`/`det_advisory_findings` in `plan_review/det_floor.py:932/942` → `partition_findings` in `orchestrator.py:380`), so the backstop must be applied AT that DET emission/aggregation point (in `det_blocking_findings`/`det_advisory_findings`, or at the `partition_findings` boundary), NOT inside `pass3_over_findings`. It never touches LLM-tier findings (they never pass through the DET emitters).
COMPONENT 3 — operator-attested AC awareness:
- Detection is pure DET (exact case-insensitive prefix per ADR 0043). INJECTION POINT: it is injected into each finding's `severity_attributes` as a boolean field `operator_attested` inside `plan_review/workflow_ops.py` `plan_review_decide` (workflow_ops.py:324 — the step that reshapes per-finding verifications into the `{index: {severity_attributes, binary}}` map Pass-3 consumes), mirroring how `code_review/workflow_ops.py:278-301` DET-enriches churn90/hard_to_reverse_surface. Pass 2 receives it as a fact, never judges it.
- Pass 1: the E2/F1/E6 rubric prompts gain the ADR-0043 vocabulary — a tagged AC is verifiable by recorded attestation; absence of in-session proving commands is not, by itself, a finding against it.
- Pass 3: deterministic suppression keyed on the DET flag. MECHANISM: `impact_plan` (decide.py) reads `attrs.get('ac_unverifiable')` and applies a 0.85 floor when it is non-none. Suppression is implemented by REMOVING (or setting to `None`) the `ac_unverifiable` key from that finding's `severity_attributes` when `operator_attested` is set — done in `plan_review_decide` while assembling the Pass-3 attrs map, BEFORE `impact_plan` reads it (not a branch inside `impact_plan`, which stays byte-unchanged). "Sole basis" = only the `ac_unverifiable` axis is cleared; every other axis (validity, other severity attrs) is untouched, so a finding that also fires another axis still survives with its non-ac_unverifiable impact. This keeps the kernel `impact_plan`/`pass3` math unchanged (the fact is injected upstream; the kernel is not taught about operator-attestation).
- Pass 4: one new coaching move — 'state the concrete attestation evidence the tagged AC will require'.
EXCLUDED (explicit non-goal): comment-trail consultation in any form — a self-refutation channel the completion verifier deliberately defends against; revisit only if post-fix field data shows surviving re-litigation friction.

## Scope
src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py + decide.py (contract binaries + the absence-claim deterministic veto — `impact_plan`/`pass3` math stays byte-unchanged), `plan_review/workflow_ops.py` (`plan_review_decide` — inject the `operator_attested` DET flag into severity_attributes and clear `ac_unverifiable` upstream of `impact_plan`), the plan-review verifier prompts (both single-turn and agentic variants), plan_review/det_floor.py (the DET-tier hygiene backstop at the DET emission/aggregation point; audit confirms P5 task-dag already names subjects), E2/F1/E6 rubric prompts, the coach MOVE_REGISTRY, tests. The f1fa 11:44 verdict (norm ne7642adbf79 + G3 n0eb3f2e9da9) becomes a regression fixture.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The verification contract carries claims_absence and absence_confirmed_in_context with abstain-safe defaults; older/absent verifiers change nothing (byte-compat test on the base model).
- [ ] Pass 3 drops a finding when claims_absence=yes and absence_confirmed_in_context=no; a regression fixture replaying the f1fa 11:44 false-absence finding is dropped, while a TRUE absence claim survives.
- [ ] The DET-scoped hygiene backstop drops a subject-less DET finding (a test feeds a synthetic subject-less DET finding through the emission/aggregation point and asserts it is dropped) and a test proves the backstop never touches LLM-tier findings. The audit of existing emitters is recorded (confirming P5 task-dag already names path+ticket subjects); any emitter found genuinely subject-less is fixed.
- [ ] The [operator-attested] tag is DET-detected and injected into severity_attributes; a tagged AC's in-session-unverifiability finding loses the ac_unverifiable hard-override contribution deterministically, with a test for a finding that still survives on other axes.
- [ ] The E2/F1/E6 rubrics and one new coaching move carry the ADR-0043 vocabulary. Proving check for "non-prescriptive": the new MOVE_REGISTRY entry passes the existing coach-move validation (bounded noun-phrase subject, template rendered deterministically) — i.e. it conforms to the same locked-registry contract every other move does (the structural guarantee of non-prescriptiveness), asserted by the MOVE_REGISTRY schema/validation test, not by subjective judgment.
- [ ] Code-review gate behavior is byte-unchanged for findings without the new binaries (shared-kernel back-compat test).
- [ ] make lint passes on the change.
- [ ] make typecheck passes on the change.
- [ ] make test passes on the change.

file_impact.reason: add abstain-safe claims_absence/absence_confirmed_in_context binaries to shared verification contract
deterministic absence-claim veto (mirror cited-reference veto); impact_plan math unchanged
inject operator_attested DET flag into severity_attributes and clear ac_unverifiable upstream of impact_plan in plan_review_decide
DET emitters name subjects; DET-tier hygiene backstop at emission point

compiled_state.title: Pass-2 grounding hardening + finding hygiene: absence-claim veto, DET content floor, operator-attested AC awareness
